data.title: What Is Going On With AMD Stock On Friday?
data.description:
• More about AMD stock moves today

The broader market remained constructive, with gains in the S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 and technology sector, indicating AMD stock’s performance reflects specific dynamics rather than a broader market-driven sell-off.

AI Demand Concerns Weigh On Sentiment

The stock’s volatility extends pressure seen earlier in the week after reports that OpenAI missed internal targets for revenue and user growth. That update renewed concerns about the pace of AI spending across the broader ecosystem. The pullback suggests investors are taking profits in a high-momentum name, even as long-term AI adoption trends remain intact.

Long-Term AI Outlook Remains Supportive

Despite near-term uncertainty, AMD highlighted stronger structural demand signals, citing an IDC white paper it sponsored. The report showed more than 80% of companies are actively planning, piloting, or deploying AI PCs, while 70% expect agentic AI systems to reshape workflows within two years.

Technical Analysis

AMD is still pinned near the top of its 52-week range, which keeps the longer-term trend pointed up even as sentiment cools. The stock is trading 25.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 54% above its 100-day SMA, a setup that indicates buyers still control both the short- and intermediate-term trend.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 79.26, which is overbought and often signals faster pullbacks when expectations wobble. RSI at 79.26 is the kind of “stretched” reading that can trigger quick profit-taking in response to small disappointments.

  • Key Resistance: $355 — Near the recent 52-week high area where rallies have been stalling.
  • Key Support: $270 — Prior dip-buying zone near the 20-day EMA area traders have been watching.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The countdown is on: Advanced Micro Devices is set to report earnings on May 5 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: $1.24 (Up from 96 cents year-over-year)
  • Revenue Estimate: $9.88 billion (Up from $7.44 billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 135.8x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy rating with a consensus price target of $293.52. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Susquehanna: Positive (Raises target to $375 on April 29)
  • Northland Capital Markets: Downgraded to Market Perform (Target $260 on April 27)
  • DA Davidson: Upgraded to Buy (Raises target to $375 on April 24)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because AMD carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

AMD Stock Price Activity: After trading lower in premarket trading, Advanced Micro Devices shares were up 0.46% to $356.11 at publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Apple Is Changing The Rules Right Before Tim Cook Exits
data.description:
iPhone Growth Is Back—But That's Not The Story

But the real shift isn't demand. It's what Apple is doing behind the scenes as Tim Cook prepares to step down, with John Ternus set to take over.

According to a report by The Information, Apple is quietly rewriting parts of its financial and strategic playbook.

Buybacks Cut, Cash Strategy Flipped

For years, Apple's capital strategy was simple: return cash aggressively and move toward ‘net cash neutral.’ That era may be ending.

The company cut stock buybacks in half in the March quarter—even as free cash flow jumped 28%. At the same time, CFO Kevan Parekh said the Cupertino, California-based company is moving away from its long-standing net cash neutral target.

The Information suggests this could signal a shift toward holding more cash, a notable break from the Cook-era discipline that prioritized massive capital returns.

Why now? One possibility is rising component costs, including memory chips. Another: optionality. As rivals pour cash into AI infrastructure, Apple may be positioning itself to stay flexible—or opportunistic.

A Late But Loud AI Signal

Then there's spending.

Apple's R&D jumped 34%, a rare acceleration for a company that has historically lagged peers like Alphabet on research intensity.

Cook framed it as investing in “opportunities” across products and services. But the timing is hard to ignore. With AI reshaping the industry, Apple appears to be stepping harder on the gas—later than some, but decisively.

Reset Before The Hand-Off

These moves are more than incremental. Together—cut buybacks, rising cash, surging R&D—they point to a company repositioning itself ahead of a leadership transition.

Cook isn't just exiting on a high note. He may be rewriting the rules before handing over the keys.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: OneMain Holdings Reports Q1 2026 Results: Full Earnings Call Transcript
data.description:
OneMain Holdings (NYSE:OMF) reported first-quarter financial results on Friday. The transcript from the company's first-quarter earnings call has been provided below.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

The full earnings call is available at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/621177414

Summary

OneMain Holdings reported strong financial results for Q1 2026, with capital generation of $194 million and C&I adjusted earnings per share of $1.95, marking a 13% increase year-over-year.

Total revenue and receivables grew by 6% year-over-year, with the company maintaining a conservative underwriting posture while expanding high-quality personal loan originations.

Strategic initiatives included enhancing personal loan offerings, leveraging AI for operational efficiency, and expanding the auto finance dealer network, contributing to robust credit performance across all business lines.

The credit card business saw significant growth, with receivables increasing 45% year-over-year and strong performance in yields and credit metrics.

Management expressed confidence in achieving 2026 financial objectives, maintaining a positive outlook despite macroeconomic uncertainties, and emphasized disciplined capital allocation with a focus on share repurchases.

We'll go first this morning to John Hett with Jefferies.

No updates this quarter; the process continues to move forward. Timing is uncertain, but we remain optimistic, as we continue to believe we have a very strong case for approval. We're having constructive dialogues with the FDIC and the Utah Department of Financial Institutions. So we're optimistic and we'll keep folks posted as, you know as things evolve.

John Hett (Equity Analyst)

Okay, thanks. And then, you know, you talked about a lot of focus on technology and, you know, using AI for productivity reasons. Any update on the branch versus Digital kind of activities and how they integrate together. And any thoughts on like, I guess, the trajectory, the branch system over time?

data.title: Senseonics Shares Sink Friday: What's Driving The Move?
data.description:
• Senseonics Holdings stock is showing downward pressure. What’s next for SENS stock?

Funding The Eversense 365 Commercial Launch

The Maryland-based medical technology company, which specializes in long-term implantable continuous glucose monitoring systems, priced 8 million shares of common stock at $5.00 per share.

In addition to the common stock, Senseonics is offering pre-funded warrants for an additional 8 million shares at a purchase price of $4.999 each. The anticipated gross proceeds of $80 million are intended to fund the ongoing commercial launch of the Eversense 365 system and the continued development of pipeline products.

Underwriters, including joint book-running managers TD Cowen and Barclays, have also been granted a 30-day option to purchase up to 2.4 million additional shares.

The offering is expected to close on May 4. While the capital infusion supports Senseonics' mission to transform glucose management for the global diabetes community, the immediate dilutive impact and offering price have triggered a significant sell-off in early Friday trading.

SENS Shares Slide Friday Morning

SENS Stock Price Activity: Senseonics Holdings shares were trading lower by 22.54% at $5.12 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning Fortinet Stock In The Last 15 Years
data.description:
Fortinet (NASDAQ:FTNT) has outperformed the market over the past 15 years by 9.48% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 21.33%. Currently, Fortinet has a market capitalization of $63.11 billion.

Buying $1000 In FTNT: If an investor had bought $1000 of FTNT stock 15 years ago, it would be worth $18,533.65 today based on a price of $86.20 for FTNT at the time of writing.

Fortinet's Performance Over Last 15 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.
